# Softwine

> Softwine is a technology consultancy founded by Bezalel Brandwine in 2024. We act as a fractional technology partner - providing strategy, execution, and team-building for startups, growing SMBs, and non-technical founders. Specializations include AI integration, cloud architecture, application and development infrastructure, software development, and engineering leadership.

Softwine helps companies make sound technical decisions without the overhead of full-time, in-house architects or DevOps teams. Our approach is to set up modern infrastructure once, properly, then maintain it efficiently - freeing teams to focus on their core business.

## Core Pages

- [Homepage](https://softwine.net/): Overview of services, specializations, target clients, and the founder's story.
- [Projects](https://softwine.net/projects-a.html): Portfolio of completed and ongoing client engagements, organized by client type (startups, SMBs, non-tech founders).
- [Blog](https://softwine.net/blog/): Articles on software architecture, AI, infrastructure, and engineering leadership.

## Services (sections of the homepage)

- [Our Specializations](https://softwine.net/index.html#services): AI integration, cloud architecture, application and development infrastructure, software development, team building and leadership.
- [Whom This Is Suitable For](https://softwine.net/index.html#suitable-for): Startups & early-stage, growing SMBs, non-tech founders.
- [Your Success, Our Mission](https://softwine.net/index.html#how-we-help): Custom AI models, AI integration, AI data pipelines, cloud migration, serverless architecture, containerization, CI/CD, DevOps, SaaS building, API design, technology team building.
- [Our Story](https://softwine.net/index.html#about): Founder background and the consultancy's origin.

## Blog Articles

- [Managed Services: The Convenience That Costs More Than You Think](https://softwine.net/blog/data-and-infrastructure/20260414-095441/en.html): Hidden costs, vendor lock-in, and knowledge gaps in managed cloud services - and what Reddit's live migration teaches about portability.
- [The Talent Tax: Why Your Back-to-Office Policy is a Gamble You Can't Afford](https://softwine.net/blog/engineering-leadership/talent-tax-wfh/en.html): How office mandates spend negotiation capital with the engineers who have the most of it.
- [Engineering Leadership: When a Title Isn't Enough and Open Source Burns Out](https://softwine.net/blog/engineering-leadership/en.html): Three lessons on leadership that creates real change - quality czars without authority, volunteer burnout, and AI's effect on open-source ownership.
- [Software Architecture: Beyond the Database Schema](https://softwine.net/blog/architecture-and-design/en.html): Distributed monoliths posing as microservices, admin-panel costs, and architecture derived from business rather than trend.
- [AI in Software Engineering: Staying the Pilot, Not Becoming the Passenger](https://softwine.net/blog/ai-and-engineering/en.html): How CTOs build teams that leverage AI without losing judgment.
- [AI Governance: When the Black Box Makes Decisions For You](https://softwine.net/blog/ai-risk-and-governance/en.html): Why AI explainability is a business problem, not just a technical one.
- [Cloud Infrastructure: Why the Cheapest Option Costs the Most](https://softwine.net/blog/data-and-infrastructure/en.html): Three infrastructure lessons about the real cost of convenience.
